root_cause: The SSE `board` handler in web/src/hooks/use-board.ts did a blind `qc.setQueryData([board,issues], snapshot)`, replacing the whole cache on every `board` event. A `board` push that arrived DURING an in-flight drag PATCH carried a server snapshot loaded BEFORE this client's move persisted (emit_board is triggered by ANY concurrent write/agent, not just our move), so it overwrote the optimistic new-column position with the stale old column => the card popped back; only a later refetch settled it. The backend persists-then-emits (server/src/board/mod.rs patch_handler: db patch at L507, emit_board at L515), so the bug is purely a client SSE-vs-optimistic race; the fix is client-side and no backend change was needed.
summary: Added a ref-held Set of issue ids with an in-flight optimistic move (patch/claim/start/create/delete), marked in each mutation's onMutate and cleared in onSettled. The SSE `board` reconciler (new reconcileBoard) now MERGES the incoming snapshot instead of clobbering: every card takes fresh server truth EXCEPT cards with a pending move, whose optimistic column/pos is preserved until that mutation's onSettled refetch reconciles it authoritatively. Concurrent live SSE deltas (agent comments/acceptance/status/moves) on OTHER cards still apply; optimistic created cards aren't dropped and optimistic deletes aren't resurrected mid-flight; drag-to-doing=Start, rollback, and reduced-motion are untouched; no timeouts. Verified: web tsc+vite build + eslint clean, full release build green; backend emit ordering confirmed against the live server; a live SSE capture showed two concurrent moves producing conflicting board frames (one `doing`, one stale `backlog`) proving the race; a 13-assertion deterministic harness against the verbatim reconcileBoard proved OLD=pop-back vs NEW=stays, plus no regression to live updates / create / delete / post-settle reconcile.
